page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-128 is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor. It features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of dedicated system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and CPU-bound processing.

Benchmark evaluations show strong multi-core capabilities, with top-tier performance in Passmark CPU and Geekbench ray tracing tests. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance fall into the top 25% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, registering strong read and write speeds for both cached and uncached blocks. However, single-core performance remains average across stress-ng, Geekbench, and compression tests.

While the server excels at parallel processing tasks like static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, it exhibits clear limitations in large-scale machine learning. LLM inference for text generation on a large 70B model is weak, though prompt processing for small and medium models remains strong. The absence of bundled local storage and public IPv4 addresses requires external provisioning. This configuration is best suited for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-128 is an x86_64 general-purpose server configured with 128 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz, it features a 54 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 vector extensions. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth, though single-core execution speeds are average. The server lacks integrated local storage and GPU accelerators, which limits its efficiency for large-scale LLM text generation. However, it demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput static web serving. This profile makes it a highly capable option for resource-dense, multi-threaded enterprise workloads that do not require local NVMe drives.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-128 is an x86_64 server with 128 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM. It delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-128 features 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A high-core-count x86_64 platform delivering balanced memory allocation and strong multi-threaded performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM on Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Top-tier performance in ray tracing and cryptographic operations
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
